name: Pinterest Pin Uploader
prompt:
Upload pins to Pinterest using browser automation. This is a MECHANICAL task — no creativity or judgment needed. Just follow the steps exactly.

If the batch has 0 pins, respond with [SILENT].

Step 1: Read credentials from the script output above (pinterest_email, pinterest_password, telegram_bot_token, telegram_chat_id, google_token_path, drive_pins_folder_id fields). Save a Telegram helper and a Drive delete helper via execute_code:
```python
import urllib.request, json

def send_tg(token, chat_id, text):
    data = json.dumps({"chat_id": chat_id, "text": text}).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request(f"https://api.telegram.org/bot{token}/sendMessage", data=data, headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"})
    urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10)

def delete_pin_from_drive(token_path, folder_id, filename):
    from google.oauth2.credentials import Credentials
    from google.auth.transport.requests import Request
    from googleapiclient.discovery import build
    with open(token_path) as f:
        td = json.load(f)
    creds = Credentials(token=td["token"], refresh_token=td["refresh_token"], token_uri=td["token_uri"], client_id=td["client_id"], client_secret=td["client_secret"], scopes=td["scopes"])
    if creds.expired:
        creds.refresh(Request())
    service = build("drive", "v3", credentials=creds)
    results = service.files().list(q=f"name='{filename}' and '{folder_id}' in parents and trashed=false", fields="files(id)").execute()
    for f in results.get("files", []):
        service.files().delete(fileId=f["id"]).execute()
    return len(results.get("files", []))
```

Step 2: Log in to Pinterest:
- browser_navigate to https://www.pinterest.com/login/
- browser_type email into the Email field
- browser_type password into the Password field
- browser_click "Log in" button
- Wait 5 seconds, take snapshot to verify login

Step 3: For each pin in the "batch" array, do exactly this:

  a. Send Telegram: "⏳ Uploading pin [N/total]: [product_name]..."

  b. Download image: use execute_code to save primary_image URL to /tmp/pin_image.jpg

  c. browser_navigate to https://www.pinterest.com/pin-creation-tool/

  d. Select board: browser_click the board dropdown, browser_type "SmartyPants9786" in the search, browser_click the board name

  e. Upload image: Use browser_click on "File upload" button, then use the file input to upload /tmp/pin_image.jpg

  f. Fill fields:
     - browser_type the "title" into the title field
     - browser_type the "description" into the description field  
     - browser_click "Add alt text", then browser_type the "alt_text"
     - browser_type the "link" (affiliate URL) into destination link field

  g. browser_click "Publish"

  h. Wait 5 seconds for confirmation

  i. If successful:
     - Send Telegram: "✅ Pin uploaded: [product_name]"
     - Update local pin file via execute_code: change status to "uploaded", add uploaded_at timestamp
     - Delete pin JSON from Google Drive via execute_code: call delete_pin_from_drive(google_token_path, drive_pins_folder_id, _file_name)
  
  j. If failed:
     - Send Telegram: "❌ Pin failed: [product_name] - [error]"
     - Update local pin file: change status to "failed", add error and failed_at

  k. Wait 5 seconds before next pin (rate limiting)

Step 4: Send Telegram: "📌 Pinterest upload batch complete: [uploaded]/[total] pins uploaded to SmartyPants9786"

Step 5: Final response — plain text summary listing each pin uploaded or failed. No markdown.
